2605.27322.title: Semantic Gradients Interactions in SSD: A Case Study in Racial Identity and Hate Speech
2605.27322.paper_summary: We introduce interaction SSD, an extension of Supervised Semantic Differential that models how semantic meaning varies across moderators such as groups, traits, or conditions making this variation testable and interpretable. The method estimates a main semantic gradient, an interaction gradient, and conditional gradients, all interpretable through standard SSD tools. We illustrate it on the UC Berkeley Measuring Hate Speech corpus, testing whether annotator racial identity moderates hate-speech judgments of comments targeting people of color. The interaction model detects a significant moderation effect: the shared gradient contrasts dehumanizing hostility with counter-speech, while the interaction gradient reveals smaller group-linked differences in which semantic cues predict hate-speech ratings. Interaction SSD makes moderated meaning-outcome relationships statistically testable and interpretable.
2605.27322.paper_summary_zh: 我們介紹互動SSD，這是監督語意差異的擴展，旨在建模語意意義如何隨著群體、特徵或條件等調節因素而變化，使這種變化可測試且可解釋。該方法估算主要的語意梯度、互動梯度和條件梯度，所有這些都可以通過標準SSD工具進行解釋。我們在加州大學伯克利分校的仇恨言論測量語料庫上進行了說明，測試標註者的種族身份是否調節針對有色人種的評論的仇恨言論判斷。互動模型檢測到顯著的調節效應：共享梯度對比去人性化的敵意與反言論，而互動梯度揭示了較小的群體相關差異，其中語意線索預測仇恨言論評分。互動SSD使得調節的意義-結果關係在統計上可測試且可解釋。
2605.27322.author: Felix Ostrowicki et.al.
